item.title: Shell injection in demo --cloud browser open: exec() interpolates chatUrl without escaping
item.intent: Browser launch must never pass user-derived or network-derived strings through a shell. execFile with argument array is the only acceptable form.
item.description: src/cli/index.ts line 420 calls exec(`${openCmd} ${chatUrl}`) where chatUrl is constructed from cloud provisioning output (IP address + port). The URL is passed directly to a shell via exec() string interpolation with no quoting or escaping. A malformed API response containing shell metacharacters could execute arbitrary commands on the user's machine. Fix: replace exec(string) with execFile(cmd, [chatUrl]) to use argument-list form, which does not invoke a shell.
acceptanceCriteria: exec() string interpolation replaced with execFile argument array in demo --cloud browser open
Browser opens correctly in demo --cloud on Linux and macOS
No shell is invoked when launching the browser URL
